  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc", "symbol": "validateHeaders"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/common/http/conn_manager_utility.cc", "symbol": "cleanInternalHeaders"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/common/http/utility.cc", "symbol": "sanitizeConnectionHeader"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/http1_header_validator.cc", "symbol": "validateRequestHeaders"},
    {"repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/http2_header_validator.cc", "symbol": "validateRequestHeaders"}
  ],
  "text": "This analysis identifies all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy (v1.31.2) that handle HTTP request header validation and injection prevention. The implementation spans multiple layers:\n\n## Core Header Validation & Utility Layer\n\n**source/common/http/header_utility.cc** - Implements critical header validation functions including `checkValidRequestHeaders()` which validates header names and values, and `checkHeaderNameForUnderscores()` which handles headers with underscores based on configuration (ALLOW, DROP_HEADER, or REJECT_REQUEST).\n\n**source/common/http/utility.cc** - Contains `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` which prevents HTTP request smuggling attacks by validating and sanitizing the Connection header. It prevents nomination of dangerous headers like X-Forwarded-* and pseudo-headers, enforces a maximum of 10 nominated headers, and validates TE header values.\n\n## Connection Manager & Request Processing\n\n**source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc** - Implements `validateHeaders()` and `validateTrailers()` methods that invoke the header validator for request headers/trailers and handle transformation results. This is the central point where header validation is enforced at the HTTP connection manager level.\n\n**source/common/http/conn_manager_utility.cc** - Implements `cleanInternalHeaders()` which strips internal-only headers from external requests to prevent header injection attacks. It removes Envoy-internal headers like x-envoy-decorator-operation, x-envoy-downstream-service-cluster, x-envoy-retry-on, x-envoy-force-trace, etc. Also implements `mutateRequestHeaders()` which handles various header mutations including x-forwarded-for, x-forwarded-host, and other transformations.\n\n## Protocol-Specific Validation\n\n**source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc** - HTTP/1 codec implementation that validates headers at the protocol level, calls `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` for connection header validation, and validates header names for forbidden characters.\n\n**source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc** - HTTP/2 codec implementation with header validation specific to HTTP/2 protocol requirements.\n\n**source/common/quic/** - QUIC stream implementations (envoy_quic_stream.h, envoy_quic_server_stream.cc, envoy_quic_utils.h) that implement header validation for QUIC protocol with methods like `validateHeader()` checking header names and values for forbidden characters.\n\n## Universal Header Validator Extension\n\n**source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/** - The Universal Header Validator (UHV) extension provides comprehensive header validation:\n\n- **header_validator.cc/h** - Base class implementing shared validation logic across HTTP versions: `validateGenericHeaderName()` and `validateGenericHeaderValue()` validate against RFC-compliant character sets, `validateMethodHeader()`, `validateStatusHeader()`, `validateSchemeHeader()`, `validateHostHeader()`, `validatePathHeaderCharacters()`, and `validateContentLengthHeader()`.\n\n- **http1_header_validator.cc/h** - HTTP/1 specific validator implementing `validateRequestHeaders()`, `validateResponseHeaders()`, `validateRequestTrailers()`, and `validateResponseTrailers()`. Includes special handling for Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length validation.\n\n- **http2_header_validator.cc/h** - HTTP/2 specific validator with protocol-specific pseudo-header validation (e.g., :method, :path, :scheme, :authority for requests; :status for responses).\n\n- **path_normalizer.cc/h** - Normalizes URL paths and validates path characters, preventing directory traversal and protocol bypass attacks.\n\n- **config.cc/h** - Factory configuration for the header validator extension.\n\n- **character_tables.h**, **config_overrides.h** - Support files for character validation and configuration override management.\n\n## Public Interfaces\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator.h** - Defines the HeaderValidator, ServerHeaderValidator, and ClientHeaderValidator interfaces that all validator implementations must follow.\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator_factory.h** - Defines the HeaderValidatorFactoryConfig factory interface.\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator_errors.h** - Defines error codes and response details for various header validation failures.\n\n## Validation Flow\n\nThe request header validation follows this flow: (1) Raw HTTP request arrives at codec layer (HTTP/1, HTTP/2, or QUIC) which performs initial protocol-specific validation. (2) Headers are then validated by the Universal Header Validator (UHV) if enabled via `validateHeaders()` in conn_manager_impl.cc. (3) `cleanInternalHeaders()` removes Envoy-internal headers from external requests. (4) `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` prevents header injection via the Connection header. (5) Route-level header transformations may add/remove headers based on configuration. This multi-layer approach ensures comprehensive defense against HTTP request header injection attacks."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Envoy HTTP Request Header Injection Validation

## Your Task

Find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that sanitize or validate HTTP request headers to prevent header injection: the header validation logic, the forbidden header list enforcement, and the internal header stripping.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the security domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367, sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6, sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2` (envoyproxy/envoy)
- `sg-evals/data-plane-api--84e84367` (envoyproxy/data-plane-api)
- `sg-evals/go-control-plane--71637ad6` (envoyproxy/go-control-plane)
- `sg-evals/grpc--957dba5e` (grpc/grpc)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy that handle HTTP request header injection validation. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for relevant code.
Now let me create the answer file with all the findings:

I've completed my analysis of the Envoy HTTP request header injection validation and created a comprehensive answer file. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Key Findings

I identified **36 C++ source files** in envoyproxy/envoy (v1.31.2) that handle HTTP request header validation and injection prevention, organized into several layers:

### Core Components:
1. **Header Validation Utilities** (header_utility.cc) - Validates header names/values and handles underscores in headers
2. **Connection Sanitization** (utility.cc) - The `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` function prevents header smuggling attacks by validating Connection header nominations
3. **Connection Manager** (conn_manager_impl.cc) - Central point for header validation enforcement
4. **Internal Header Stripping** (conn_manager_utility.cc) - The `cleanInternalHeaders()` function removes Envoy-internal headers from external requests to prevent injection

### Protocol-Specific Implementation:
- HTTP/1 codec validation
- HTTP/2 codec validation  
- QUIC stream validation

### Universal Header Validator Extension:
A comprehensive set of files in `source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/` that provides:
- RFC-compliant character validation
- HTTP-version-specific validation
- Path normalization
- Method and status validation

### Key Validation Functions:
- `checkValidRequestHeaders()` - Core validation
- `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` - Prevents header injection via Connection header
- `cleanInternalHeaders()` - Strips internal headers from external requests
- `validateHeaders()` / `validateTrailers()` - Connection manager validation

The answer file has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with detailed documentation including symbols, validation chain, and a comprehensive narrative explanation.

  "text": "This analysis identifies all C++ source files in envoyproxy/envoy (v1.31.2) that handle HTTP request header validation and injection prevention. The implementation spans multiple layers:\n\n## Core Header Validation & Utility Layer\n\n**source/common/http/header_utility.cc** - Implements critical header validation functions including `checkValidRequestHeaders()` which validates header names and values, and `checkHeaderNameForUnderscores()` which handles headers with underscores based on configuration (ALLOW, DROP_HEADER, or REJECT_REQUEST).\n\n**source/common/http/utility.cc** - Contains `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` which prevents HTTP request smuggling attacks by validating and sanitizing the Connection header. It prevents nomination of dangerous headers like X-Forwarded-* and pseudo-headers, enforces a maximum of 10 nominated headers, and validates TE header values.\n\n## Connection Manager & Request Processing\n\n**source/common/http/conn_manager_impl.cc** - Implements `validateHeaders()` and `validateTrailers()` methods that invoke the header validator for request headers/trailers and handle transformation results. This is the central point where header validation is enforced at the HTTP connection manager level.\n\n**source/common/http/conn_manager_utility.cc** - Implements `cleanInternalHeaders()` which strips internal-only headers from external requests to prevent header injection attacks. It removes Envoy-internal headers like x-envoy-decorator-operation, x-envoy-downstream-service-cluster, x-envoy-retry-on, x-envoy-force-trace, etc. Also implements `mutateRequestHeaders()` which handles various header mutations including x-forwarded-for, x-forwarded-host, and other transformations.\n\n## Protocol-Specific Validation\n\n**source/common/http/http1/codec_impl.cc** - HTTP/1 codec implementation that validates headers at the protocol level, calls `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` for connection header validation, and validates header names for forbidden characters.\n\n**source/common/http/http2/codec_impl.cc** - HTTP/2 codec implementation with header validation specific to HTTP/2 protocol requirements.\n\n**source/common/quic/** - QUIC stream implementations (envoy_quic_stream.h, envoy_quic_server_stream.cc, envoy_quic_utils.h) that implement header validation for QUIC protocol with methods like `validateHeader()` checking header names and values for forbidden characters.\n\n## Universal Header Validator Extension\n\n**source/extensions/http/header_validators/envoy_default/** - The Universal Header Validator (UHV) extension provides comprehensive header validation:\n\n- **header_validator.cc/h** - Base class implementing shared validation logic across HTTP versions: `validateGenericHeaderName()` and `validateGenericHeaderValue()` validate against RFC-compliant character sets, `validateMethodHeader()`, `validateStatusHeader()`, `validateSchemeHeader()`, `validateHostHeader()`, `validatePathHeaderCharacters()`, and `validateContentLengthHeader()`.\n\n- **http1_header_validator.cc/h** - HTTP/1 specific validator implementing `validateRequestHeaders()`, `validateResponseHeaders()`, `validateRequestTrailers()`, and `validateResponseTrailers()`. Includes special handling for Transfer-Encoding and Content-Length validation.\n\n- **http2_header_validator.cc/h** - HTTP/2 specific validator with protocol-specific pseudo-header validation (e.g., :method, :path, :scheme, :authority for requests; :status for responses).\n\n- **path_normalizer.cc/h** - Normalizes URL paths and validates path characters, preventing directory traversal and protocol bypass attacks.\n\n- **config.cc/h** - Factory configuration for the header validator extension.\n\n- **character_tables.h**, **config_overrides.h** - Support files for character validation and configuration override management.\n\n## Public Interfaces\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator.h** - Defines the HeaderValidator, ServerHeaderValidator, and ClientHeaderValidator interfaces that all validator implementations must follow.\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator_factory.h** - Defines the HeaderValidatorFactoryConfig factory interface.\n\n**envoy/http/header_validator_errors.h** - Defines error codes and response details for various header validation failures.\n\n## Validation Flow\n\nThe request header validation follows this flow: (1) Raw HTTP request arrives at codec layer (HTTP/1, HTTP/2, or QUIC) which performs initial protocol-specific validation. (2) Headers are then validated by the Universal Header Validator (UHV) if enabled via `validateHeaders()` in conn_manager_impl.cc. (3) `cleanInternalHeaders()` removes Envoy-internal headers from external requests. (4) `sanitizeConnectionHeader()` prevents header injection via the Connection header. (5) Route-level header transformations may add/remove headers based on configuration. This multi-layer approach ensures comprehensive defense against HTTP request header injection attacks."
}
